[30;1m544 | [0m[35m<h2 id="literals">Extensions to the Lua Parser</h2>[0m
[30;1m545 | [0m[35m<p>[0m
[30;1m546 | [0m[35mThe parser for Lua source code treats numeric literals with the[0m
[30;1m547 | [0m[35msuffixes <tt>LL</tt> or <tt>ULL</tt> as signed or unsigned 64&nbsp;bit[0m
[30;1m548 | [0m[35mintegers. Case doesn't matter, but uppercase is recommended for[0m
[30;1m549 | [0m[35mreadability. It handles decimal (<tt>42LL</tt>), hexadecimal[0m

[30;1m1062 | [0m[35mFor new designs <b>avoid push-style APIs</b>: a C&nbsp;function repeatedly[0m
[30;1m1063 | [0m[35mcalling a callback for each result. Instead <b>use pull-style APIs</b>:[0m
[30;1m1064 | [0m[35mcall a C&nbsp;function repeatedly to get a new result. Calls from Lua[0m
[30;1m1065 | [0m[35mto C via the FFI are much faster than the other way round. Most well-designed[0m
[30;1m1066 | [0m[35mlibraries already use pull-style APIs (read/write, get/put).[0m
[30;1m1067 | [0m[35m</p>[0m

[30;1m1034 | [0m[35m<h3 id="callback_performance">Callback performance</h3>[0m
[30;1m1035 | [0m[35m<p>[0m
[30;1m1036 | [0m[35m<b>Callbacks are slow!</b> First, the C&nbsp;to Lua transition itself[0m
[30;1m1037 | [0m[35mhas an unavoidable cost, similar to a <tt>lua_call()</tt> or[0m
[30;1m1038 | [0m[35m<tt>lua_pcall()</tt>. Argument and result marshalling add to that cost.[0m
[30;1m1039 | [0m[35mAnd finally, neither the C&nbsp;compiler nor LuaJIT can inline or[0m

[30;1m1028 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m1029 | [0m[35mcb:free()[0m
[30;1m1030 | [0m[35m-- The callback function pointer is no longer valid and its resources[0m
[30;1m1031 | [0m[35m-- will be reclaimed. The created Lua closure will be garbage collected.[0m

[30;1m343 | [0m[35mNote that since the function returns now, the <tt>buf</tt> and[0m
[30;1m344 | [0m[35m<tt>buflen</tt> variables will eventually be garbage collected. This[0m
[30;1m345 | [0m[35mis fine, because <tt>ffi.string()</tt> has copied the contents to a[0m
[30;1m346 | [0m[35mnewly created (interned) Lua string. If you plan to call this function[0m
[30;1m347 | [0m[35mlots of times, consider reusing the buffers and/or handing back the[0m
[30;1m348 | [0m[35mresults in buffers instead of strings. This will reduce the overhead[0m
[30;1m349 | [0m[35mfor garbage collection and string interning.[0m

[30;1m81 | [0m[35mas a separate module). The code generated by the JIT-compiler for[0m
